name: Puertos de Navafría, Cotos y Navacerrada - Parque Nacional de la Sierra de Guadarrama — circular desde Segovia
region_intro: Road cycling around Segovia offers diverse terrain, from the challenging climbs of the Sierra de Guadarrama mountains to the rolling hills and river valleys of the rural countryside. The region is characterized by its proximity to the Sistema Central mountain range, providing significant elevation gains and scenic mountain passes. Cyclists can also explore picturesque river valleys like the Eresma and Duratón, alongside vast cereal plains and ancient oak and pine forests. This varied landscape provides a range of road cycling experiences for different skill levels. Best road cycling routes in Segovia The most popular road cycling route is Puertos de Navafría, Cotos y Navacerrada - Parque Nacional de la Sierra de Guadarrama — circular desde Segovia, a 78.9 miles (127.0 km) trail that takes 6 hours 44 minutes to complete. This difficult route navigates through the Sierra de Guadarrama National Park, featuring multiple mountain passes. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Segovia - Navacerrada Pass Loop, a difficult 35.4 miles (57.0 km) path. It offers significant elevation gain as it ascends towards the Navacerrada Pass, providing expansive mountain views. Local road cyclists also love the Alto del León and Puerto de Navacerrada Loop, a 59.6 miles (95.9 km) trail leading through the high mountain passes of Alto del León and Puerto de Navacerrada, often completed in about 5 hours 8 minutes. Road cycling around Segovia is defined by the challenging ascents of the Sierra de Guadarrama, the scenic Eresma Valley, and routes passing through historic medieval towns. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult rides, catering to various ability levels. The routes in Segovia are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 60 reviews. More than 3,000 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Segovia's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect when road cycling in Segovia?
A: Segovia offers a diverse range of terrain for road cyclists. You can expect challenging climbs in the &lt;strong&gt;Sierra de Guadarrama mountains&lt;/strong&gt;, picturesque river valleys like the Eresma and Duratón, and rolling hills across vast cereal plains. The landscape transitions from mountainous foothills to open countryside, providing varied experiences for all skill levels.

Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Segovia?
A: There are over 140 road cycling routes available around Segovia, catering to various preferences and skill levels. These routes have been explored and rated by thousands of komoot users.

Q: Are there road cycling routes suitable for beginners in Segovia?
A: Yes, Segovia offers several road cycling routes suitable for beginners. There are 12 easy routes available, allowing newer cyclists to enjoy the region's beauty without overly strenuous climbs. These routes often feature gentler gradients and shorter distances.

Q: What are the most challenging road cycling routes in Segovia?
A: For experienced road cyclists seeking a challenge, Segovia offers 37 difficult routes, many of which involve significant elevation gains in the Sierra de Guadarrama. Notable challenging routes include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1063897154/puertos-de-navafria-cotos-y-navacerrada-parque-nacional-de-la-sierra-de-guadarrama-circular-desde-segovia" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puertos de Navafría, Cotos y Navacerrada — circular desde Segovia&lt;/a&gt;, which covers nearly 127 km with over 2,300 meters of ascent, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1063924140/alto-del-leon-and-puerto-de-navacerrada-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alto del León and Puerto de Navacerrada Loop&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What historical landmarks can I see while road cycling near Segovia?
A: Many road cycling routes in Segovia pass by or near significant historical landmarks. You can cycle past the monumental &lt;strong&gt;Roman Aqueduct&lt;/strong&gt; in Segovia, explore charming medieval towns like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5784283" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pedraza&lt;/a&gt;, or visit the stunning &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1814018" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alcázar of Segovia&lt;/a&gt;. The region also features royal sites like the Royal Palace of San Ildefonso (La Granja) and numerous Romanesque churches.

Q: Are there scenic viewpoints along the road cycling routes in Segovia?
A: Absolutely. Road cycling routes in Segovia offer numerous scenic viewpoints, especially those ascending into the Sierra de Guadarrama. You'll find expansive mountain vistas from passes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4225665" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Navacerrada Pass&lt;/a&gt;, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4220056" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alto del León&lt;/a&gt;, and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4003184" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Navafría Pass&lt;/a&gt;. The Pradera de San Marcos also offers picturesque views of Segovia's iconic monuments.

Q: Can I combine road cycling with exploring natural features in Segovia?
A: Yes, Segovia's road cycling routes often integrate stunning natural features. You can ride alongside the Duratón River in the &lt;strong&gt;Duratón Canyon Nature Reserve&lt;/strong&gt;, known for its dramatic limestone gorges and impressive birds of prey like griffon vultures. The Eresma Valley also provides a beautiful backdrop with its well-maintained routes, oak groves, and pine forests.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Segovia?
A: The best times for road cycling in Segovia are typically spring (April-June) and fall (September-October). During these seasons, the weather is mild and pleasant, making for ideal cycling conditions. Summers can be hot, especially in the plains, while winters can bring colder temperatures and snow in the higher mountain passes.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes in Segovia?
A: Many of Segovia's road cycling routes are designed as circular loops, allowing you to start and end your ride in the same location. Examples include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1063897154/puertos-de-navafria-cotos-y-navacerrada-parque-nacional-de-la-sierra-de-guadarrama-circular-desde-segovia" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puertos de Navafría, Cotos y Navacerrada — circular desde Segovia&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1064521423/pedraza-and-by-the-rio-cega-circular-by-cantalejo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pedraza and by the Río Cega — circular by Cantalejo&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy the most about road cycling in Segovia?
A: The road cycling experience in Segovia is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 60 reviews. Cyclists often praise the challenging ascents of the Sierra de Guadarrama, the scenic beauty of the Eresma Valley, and the opportunity to pass through historic medieval towns. The varied terrain and well-maintained roads are frequently highlighted.

Q: Are there options for family-friendly road cycling in Segovia?
A: While many routes in Segovia are challenging, there are easier options that can be more suitable for families or those looking for a relaxed ride. The 12 easy routes available often feature less elevation and quieter roads, making them more accessible for a family outing. Look for routes that stay within the river valleys or flatter plains.

Q: Can I find cafes or restaurants along road cycling routes in Segovia?
A: Yes, many road cycling routes in Segovia pass through or near charming towns and villages where you can find cafes and restaurants. Historic towns like Pedraza and Sepulveda, or even smaller villages, offer opportunities for a coffee stop or a meal. Routes through the Eresma Valley also have options for refreshments.

Q: Is public transport an option for reaching road cycling routes in Segovia?
A: Segovia is well-connected by public transport, particularly by train and bus from Madrid. While bringing bikes on all public transport might have restrictions, local buses or regional trains often accommodate bicycles, especially outside of peak hours. It's advisable to check specific transport provider policies in advance for carrying bikes.

Q: Are there any specific permits required for road cycling in Segovia?
A: Generally, no specific permits are required for road cycling on public roads in the Segovia region. However, if your route takes you through protected areas like the Sierra de Guadarrama National Park or the Duratón Canyon Nature Reserve, it's always good practice to be aware of any specific regulations regarding access or activities, though road cycling on designated routes is usually permitted.
tips: On the list of the ''first'' riders in Madrid, Navafría is the one most enjoyed by the cyclist on foot. It's a cool, shady, twisty, tough, quiet route.

The history of this monastery can be grouped around three main axes: El Paular-Cartuja (1390-1835), El Paular disentailed (1835-1954) and El Paular recovered for monastic life (1954-until today).
huerta_paularThe foundation of the Charterhouse of Santa María de El Paular dates back to 1390. The House of Trastamara granted it great privileges and defrayed the costs of the works throughout the reign. Juan I began to erect the first Cartuja de Castilla, where there were already some hunting palaces that are cited in Alfonso X's Book of Falconry and a Hermitage called Santa María del Pobolar. This is how the first Cartuja de Castilla was built and the sixth of the Carthusian foundations in Spain.

This well-known port in the area has a length of more than 15 kilometers. The first part is smooth and stretched out and the second very constant and homogeneous, with slopes between 6% and 7%. We catalog the port as 2nd Category.
From the SG-615 road, at the height of the Navacerrada pass, you can see the entire Valsaín valley and, in the background, the Castilian plateau that is lost on the horizon.

The Siete Revueltas are one of the most iconic sections of the road that ascends to the Navacerrada Pass.

This section consists of seven hairpin bends, one after the other, marking the final and toughest part of the climb from the Segovia side.
